page: Microsoft Azure DC16s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the DCSv3 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor is equipped with 48 KB of L1d cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX-512 VNNI for vector neural network instructions, AES cryptography, and Intel SHA extensions. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

In benchmark evaluations, the DC16s_v3 demonstrates solid mid-range capabilities. Its single-core stress-ng performance scores 1898.0, while multi-core stress-ng reaches 30343.46. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 1687.72 GB/sec, dropping to 143.02 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, and a write speed of 60.51 GB/sec. The instance exhibits strong performance in specific tasks, notably image processing with a Geekbench score of 13806.0 and small-scale LLM inference, achieving 2293.61 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a 135M parameter model. However, performance scales down significantly on larger models, with a weak prompt processing speed of 6.61 tokens/sec on a 70B parameter model, highlighting a hardware limitation for heavy AI workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the DC16s_v3 offers a balanced profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU acceleration. The 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it highly suitable for memory-optimized tasks without the premium cost of GPU-enabled hardware. It is well-suited for database operations, software compilation, medium-scale web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure DC16s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the DCSv3 family, powered by a 16-core Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Featuring 128.0 GB of RAM, it provides a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio, making it highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads. The x86_64 architecture supports AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration, though it lacks local storage and GPU resources. Benchmarks indicate average multi-core performance and strong results in image processing and small-scale LLM inference, though it struggles with larger AI models. This instance is best suited for database operations, software compilation, and general-purpose memory-heavy applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC16s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and AVX-512 support, delivering balanced performance for database and memory-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DC16s_v3 is a memory-optimized VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, ideal for database and memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring sixteen dedicated Intel Xeon cores and high memory density for secure, database-driven workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
AVX-512 and cryptographic instruction set acceleration
Strong performance in image processing and small-scale LLM inference
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage
Optimized for database operations, software compilation, and memory-heavy tasks
